title: gastrorchis lutea subsp. longibracteata
intro: Gastrorchis lutea subsp. longibracteata, commonly known as the golden orchid, is a rare and endemic species of orchid found in the mountainous regions of Madagascar. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood sugar levels and alleviate symptoms of diabetes. The health benefits of Gastrorchis lutea subsp. longibracteata include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which can help to protect against various diseases and promote overall well-being. Gastrorchis lutea subsp. longibracteata is typically prepared as an herbal tea or infusion by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water, or as a tincture by extracting its active compounds in a solvent.
